text: I spent months planning a trip to Japan, researching the best places to visit and booking accommodation. When I finally arrived, I was amazed by the new culture—polite people, peaceful temples, and busy city streets. Trying local food was unforgettable; I loved fresh sushi and warm ramen, though some dishes surprised me. Language barriers were sometimes challenging, as I only knew a few phrases, but people helped with gestures. Now, those memories—of smiling strangers, tasty meals, and funny misunderstandings—stay with me, reminding me how travel opens your eyes to the world.
